hooks.command: echo 'ORCHESTRATOR REMINDER: You are the orchestrator. Delegate all substantive work to agent teammates: implementer (code, plan mode), architect (design review), reviewer (code review), test-writer (tests). Create a team, assign tasks, coordinate. Only make trivial edits yourself if the user explicitly agrees.'

hooks.prompt:
Analyze this user prompt: $ARGUMENTS

You are evaluating whether the orchestrator should delegate this to an agent team. Code changes → implementer (plan mode). Architecture → architect. Code review → reviewer. Tests → test-writer. Simple questions or explicit direct-action requests do not need delegation. Always allow the prompt. Respond with JSON: {"ok": true, "reason": "Delegate to [agent] for [task]"} or {"ok": true, "reason": "Simple question, handle directly"}

Evaluate this Write tool call: $ARGUMENTS

Check: (1) Is a new file truly necessary or should an existing file be edited? (2) Does it respect crate dependency flow (cli/server → analyzer → core)? (3) If in crates/core/, no platform-specific deps (must compile to WASM). (4) If new public API/trait/data model — has design been validated? Respond with JSON: {"ok": true, "reason": "checks pass"} or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific concern"}

Evaluate this Edit tool call: $ARGUMENTS

Check: (1) Has the file been read first? (2) Does the change maintain crate dependency flow (cli/server → analyzer → core)? (3) If crates/core/, preserves WASM compatibility? (4) If changing public API/data model — covered by existing design? (5) Follows existing codebase patterns? Respond with JSON: {"ok": true, "reason": "checks pass"} or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific concern"}

Evaluate this completed Edit/Write: $ARGUMENTS

Check: (1) Does modified code need tests? (2) Any unwrap()/expect() outside test modules? (3) Public API missing doc comments? (4) Security concerns (path traversal, injection, unsafe)? Respond with JSON: {"ok": true, "reason": "checks pass"} or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific concern"}
